# Data Model DPDCA Workflow
# EVA-STORY: F37-DPDCA-001

Version: 1.0.0
Updated: 2026-03-01
Project: 37-data-model -- EVA Data Model API

This document defines the end-to-end DPDCA execution loop for 37-data-model.
Every session, sprint, and agent run follows this pattern exactly.
The loop is content-agnostic -- it applies to all phases, all epics, all story sizes.

---

## The ID Consistency Problem (why this exists)

IDs are generated once during seeding but were never written back to source.
Agents and humans had to guess IDs, producing invented strings like F37-03-021
that have no entry in veritas-plan.json. This document closes the loop.

The canonical ID lifecycle:

  1. Author writes PLAN.md with WBS story text (no IDs required yet)
  2. seed-from-plan.py assigns canonical IDs and writes veritas-plan.json
  3. reflect-ids.py writes [F37-NN-NNN] back into every story line in PLAN.md
  4. PLAN.md is now the human-readable canonical ID reference
  5. Every agent reads PLAN.md -- IDs are right there -- no guessing needed
  6. Sprint manifests are generated by gen-sprint-manifest.py from those IDs
  7. Agent writes code using correct IDs from manifest and PLAN.md
  8. Veritas audit finds matching EVA-STORY tags and awards coverage points

---

## DPDCA Loop (repeat for every sprint)

=======================================================================
D -- DISCOVER
=======================================================================

Purpose: synthesise current state before deciding what to do next.

Step D.1 -- Read governance docs (in order)
  cat README.md PLAN.md STATUS.md ACCEPTANCE.md

Step D.2 -- Read latest findings (if exists)
  Get-ChildItem docs -Filter "*findings*" -Name | Sort-Object -Descending | Select-Object -First 1

Step D.3 -- Check data model health
  $base = "https://msub-eva-data-model.victoriousgrass-30debbd3.canadacentral.azurecontainerapps.io"
  Invoke-RestMethod "$base/health" | Select-Object status, store, version
  Invoke-RestMethod "$base/model/agent-summary" | Select-Object total

Step D.4 -- Run Veritas audit to get the true progress baseline
  node C:\AICOE\eva-foundry\48-eva-veritas\src\cli.js audit --repo . --warn-only 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 20

Step D.5 -- Write the Session Brief (one paragraph):
  Active phase, current MTI, last test count, next undone story, open blockers.
  Block on any FAIL gate before proceeding.

=======================================================================
P -- PLAN
=======================================================================

Purpose: select the next story and generate a sprint manifest.
Never start implementing before P is complete.

Step P.1 -- Add / update stories in PLAN.md if needed
  Edit PLAN.md to add new stories in WBS format:
    Story N.M.K  As a <role> I want to <action> so that <outcome>
  OLD format only -- IDs will be added by reflect-ids.py in step P.3.

Step P.2 -- Reseed veritas
  C:\AICOE\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/seed-from-plan.py --reseed-model
  Expected: "[PASS] N objects written | DB active total: M"

Step P.3 -- Reflect IDs back into PLAN.md  (THE CRITICAL STEP)
  C:\AICOE\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/reflect-ids.py
  Expected: "[PASS] Annotated N story lines in PLAN.md"
  After this step every story line looks like:
    Story 2.5.4 [F37-02-017]  As the system, after mark_collection_complete...
  PLAN.md is now the canonical ID reference.  No other source is needed.
  Commit PLAN.md + veritas-plan.json together.

Step P.4 -- Browse undone stories
  C:\AICOE\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/gen-sprint-manifest.py --list-undone
  Find the next unchecked story or group of stories for this sprint.

Step P.5 -- Generate sprint manifest
  C:\AICOE\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/gen-sprint-manifest.py `
    --sprint 02 `
    --name "fk-phase0" `
    --stories F37-FK-001,F37-FK-002,F37-FK-003 `
    --sizes F37-FK-001=M,F37-FK-002=XS,F37-FK-003=XS
  Output: .github/sprints/sprint-02-fk-phase0.md with SPRINT_MANIFEST JSON stub

Step P.6 -- Fill in TODO fields
  Open the generated sprint manifest and fill in:
  - files_to_create   (list of paths the agent should write)
  - acceptance        (concrete, testable criteria -- no vague language)
  - implementation_notes  (enough context for the agent to not guess)

Step P.7 -- Commit sprint manifest
  git add PLAN.md .eva/veritas-plan.json .github/sprints/sprint-NN-name.md
  git commit -m "feat(F37-DPDCA-001): sprint-NN manifest + PLAN.md IDs reflected"

=======================================================================
D -- DO
=======================================================================

Purpose: execute the sprint.  Automated agent writes code.

Step D.6 -- Create GitHub issue to trigger sprint-agent.yml
  gh issue create `
    --repo eva-foundry/37-data-model `
    --title "[SPRINT-02] fk-phase0" `
    --body-file .github/sprints/sprint-02-fk-phase0.md `
    --label "sprint-task"

  The sprint-agent.yml workflow fires on label "sprint-task".
  sprint_agent.py reads the SPRINT_MANIFEST JSON block from the issue body.

Step D.7 -- Agent execution (automated -- do not interrupt)
  The agent:
    a. Reads the SPRINT_MANIFEST JSON from the issue body
    b. Calls _load_context() -- loads copilot-instructions.md, PLAN.md, STATUS.md,
       FK Enhancement design docs, USER-GUIDE.md
    c. For each story (in order defined in the manifest):
       - Calls _load_story_files(story) -- loads existing file content
       - Calls gpt-4o (M/L) or gpt-4o-mini (XS/S) with full context + story requirements
       - Writes the response to the target files
       - Tags every file with correct EVA-STORY: F37-NN-NNN (from manifest -- not invented)
       - Commits with subject "feat(F37-NN-NNN): <description>"
    d. Opens a PR when done

  Model: gpt-4o for M/L stories, gpt-4o-mini for XS/S (verified available via
  GITHUB_TOKEN at models.inference.ai.azure.com -- claude-* NOT available).

  EVA-STORY tag format reminder (by file type):
    # EVA-STORY: F37-NN-NNN      Python, YAML, Dockerfile, .env files
    // EVA-STORY: F37-NN-NNN     JS/TS, Bicep files
    <!-- EVA-STORY: F37-NN-NNN   HTML, React JSX/TSX files (not used in 37-data-model)

=======================================================================
C -- CHECK
=======================================================================

Purpose: verify the work before merging.

Step C.1 -- Run tests
  C:\AICOE\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/ -x -q 2>&1
  Must exit 0.

Step C.2 -- Run Veritas audit
  node C:\AICOE\eva-foundry\48-eva-veritas\src\cli.js audit --repo . 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 20
  MTI must be >= 95 (production gate -- data model is foundational infrastructure).
  Check trust.json: "no-deploy" flag must not be present.

Step C.3 -- EVA-STORY tag coverage check
  grep -r "EVA-STORY:" --include="*.py" --include="*.json" -l
  Every file modified in the PR must appear in this list.

Step C.4 -- Commit format check
  git log --oneline -10
  Every story commit must have format: "type(F37-NN-NNN): description"
  Numeric IDs only -- alpha IDs (F37-STATS-001) are not mined by Veritas.

Step C.5 -- Review findings (optional but recommended)
  Trigger sonnet-review by adding label "sonnet-review" to the sprint issue.
  Read the generated *review_findings* doc for real violations before merging.

Step C.6 -- Validate model (critical for 37-data-model)
  $base = "https://msub-eva-data-model.victoriousgrass-30debbd3.canadacentral.azurecontainerapps.io"
  $v = Invoke-RestMethod "$base/model/admin/validate" -Headers @{"Authorization"="Bearer dev-admin"}
  $v.count       # must be 0
  $v.violations  # must be []

=======================================================================
A -- ACT
=======================================================================

Purpose: record results and close the loop.

Step A.1 -- Mark done stories in PLAN.md
  For each story shipped: add "  Status: DONE" line under the story in PLAN.md.
  OR add F37-NN-NNN to the "Story ID Roster" section.

Step A.2 -- Reseed + reflect
  C:\AICOE\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/seed-from-plan.py --reseed-model
  C:\AICOE\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/reflect-ids.py
  Veritas will now show those stories as done=true.

Step A.3 -- Update STATUS.md
  Record: sprint closed, test count, MTI, next sprint. Update "last session" heading.

Step A.4 -- Update ACCEPTANCE.md
  Check off acceptance gates that are now passing.

Step A.5 -- Export to Cosmos (37-data-model specific)
  $c = Invoke-RestMethod "$base/model/admin/commit" -Method POST -Headers @{"Authorization"="Bearer dev-admin"}
  $c.status          # "PASS" (ACA: check violation_count=0 and exported_total match)
  $c.violation_count # 0 = clean
  $c.exported_total  # must match agent-summary.total

Step A.6 -- Commit act artifacts
  git add PLAN.md .eva/veritas-plan.json STATUS.md ACCEPTANCE.md model/
  git commit -m "chore(F37-DPDCA-001): sprint-NN act artifacts -- MTI N, N tests"
  git push origin main

Return to D -- DISCOVER for the next sprint.

---

## Memory Management (Checkpoints & Continuation)

Purpose: preserve context across sessions to prevent re-discovery overhead.

### When to Create Memory Checkpoints

Create memory checkpoint file in `/memories/session/` at these DPDCA boundary transitions:

1. **DISCOVER → PLAN**: Document what was discovered (baseline state, current layer count, governance status)
2. **PLAN → DO**: Document the plan (what will be built, commit sequence, expected deltas)
3. **DO → CHECK**: Document implementation results (files created, tests added, actual deltas)
4. **CHECK → ACT**: Document validation results (test counts, MTI scores, production readiness)
5. **ACT → Next Cycle**: Document completion status (what shipped, next priorities, blockers)

### Memory Checkpoint Template

```markdown
# [Phase Name] - [Status]

**Date**: [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM TZ]
**Session**: [Session number + part]
**Branch**: [feature-branch-name]
**Status**: [Complete/In Progress/Blocked]

## What Was Completed
- [Deliverable 1 with evidence]
- [Deliverable 2 with evidence]

## Current State
- Branch: [branch-name]
- Commits: [commit-hash] (N commits)
- Files changed: [count] ([list key files])
- Layer count: [baseline] → [current]
- Tests: [N/M passing]

## Next Actions
1. [Next step 1 with clear acceptance criteria]
2. [Next step 2 with clear acceptance criteria]

## Lessons Learned (capture immediately)
- [What worked well - specific pattern/tool/approach]
- [What didn't work - root cause + corrective action]
- [Unexpected discovery - impact + documentation location]

## Blockers & Risks
- [Blocker 1: description + mitigation]
- [Risk 1: impact + monitoring plan]

## Context for Next Agent
[1-2 paragraphs: where we are, what's ready, what to do next]
```

### Memory Checkpoint Locations

Store memory files in `/memories/session/` with these naming conventions:

- **Phase checkpoints**: `phase-[N]-[name]-[status].md`
  - Example: `phase-3-execution-layers-discovery.md`
  - Example: `phase-3-execution-layers-complete.md`

- **Session checkpoints**: `session-[N]-part-[M]-[status].md`
  - Example: `session-41-part-9-completion.md`
  - Example: `session-41-part-10-blocked.md`

- **Critical incidents**: `incident-[date]-[brief-name].md`
  - Example: `incident-2026-03-09-github-auth-failure.md`

### Bootstrap Pattern (New Agent Session)

When starting a new session, bootstrap in this order:

```powershell
# Step 1: Read most recent session checkpoint
ls /memories/session/ | Sort-Object -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
# Read that file to understand current state

# Step 2: Bootstrap from data model API
$base = "https://msub-eva-data-model.victoriousgrass-30debbd3.canadacentral.azurecontainerapps.io"
$guide = Invoke-RestMethod "$base/model/agent-guide"
# This returns query patterns, write cycle rules, common mistakes, available layers

# Step 3: Get current baseline
$summary = Invoke-RestMethod "$base/model/agent-summary"
# Record layer count, evidence count - this is your DISCOVER baseline

# Step 4: Read project governance docs (in order)
cat README.md PLAN.md STATUS.md ACCEPTANCE.md

# Step 5: Check git state
git status
git branch -a
git log --oneline -5
# Understand which branch you're on and what was last committed
```

**Total bootstrap time**: <60 seconds (vs 10-30 minutes re-discovering everything)

### Memory vs Documentation

**Memory** (session checkpoints):
- Temporary working state
- Current blockers & risks
- "What I'm doing right now"
- Ephemeral (deleted after session concludes)

**Documentation** (docs/sessions/, docs/library/):
- Permanent knowledge
- Lessons learned (what worked/didn't)
- "What we learned that applies to future work"
- Archived (never deleted, moves to docs/sessions/)

**Pattern**: Write memory checkpoint immediately (5 min), consolidate to documentation in ACT phase (20 min).

### Critical: Memory Refresh in Long Sessions

For sessions >2 hours or >20 tool calls, create intermediate checkpoints:

1. **Every major sub-phase completion** (e.g., "schemas done", "tests done")
2. **Before context switches** (e.g., switching from development to git operations)
3. **After resolving blockers** (e.g., "GitHub auth fixed - ready to retry PR")
4. **Before asking user for input** (preserve state in case session pauses)

**Why**: LLM context windows can overflow. Memory checkpoints let new agent pick up exactly where you left off.

### Memory Checkpoint Anti-Patterns

❌ **Don't**: Write checkpoint after entire DPDCA cycle (too late - context already lost)  
✅ **Do**: Write checkpoint at EACH phase boundary (D→P, P→D, D→C, C→A)

❌ **Don't**: Write "we did some stuff" (vague, not actionable)  
✅ **Do**: Write "created 4 schemas (433 lines), committed as a5ec5b5" (specific, verifiable)

❌ **Don't**: Skip lessons learned section ("will document later")  
✅ **Do**: Capture lessons **immediately** (fresh in context, details accurate)

❌ **Don't**: Store memory in project files (mixes working state with permanent docs)  
✅ **Do**: Store memory in `/memories/session/` (clear separation, easy cleanup)

### Evidence: Session 41 Memory Pattern Success

Session 41 spanned 6 hours with 4 memory checkpoints:
1. Part 9 completion (5 DPDCA cycles)
2. Phase 3 discovery (design specs read)
3. Phase 3 plan (schemas + test architecture)
4. Phase 3 complete (ready for PR)

**Result**:
- Zero context loss across 2 major transitions
- New agent resumed from checkpoint in <5 min (vs 30 min re-discovery)
- No duplicate work (exact file list preserved)
- Clear continuation path (next actions documented)

**Template Location**: This pattern is now the standard for all future sessions.

---

## Quick Reference: Command Cheat Sheet

| Step | Command |
|------|---------|
| Add stories | Edit PLAN.md: Story N.M.K  <title> |
| Reseed | python scripts/seed-from-plan.py --reseed-model |
| Reflect IDs | python scripts/reflect-ids.py |
| Browse undone | python scripts/gen-sprint-manifest.py --list-undone |
| Generate manifest | python scripts/gen-sprint-manifest.py --sprint NN --name X --stories F37-NN-NNN,... |
| Run tests | C:\AICOE\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/ -x -q |
| Veritas audit | node C:\AICOE\eva-foundry\48-eva-veritas\src\cli.js audit --repo . |
| Validate model | Invoke-RestMethod "$base/model/admin/validate" -Headers @{"Authorization"="Bearer dev-admin"} |
| Create issue | gh issue create --repo eva-foundry/37-data-model --title "[SPRINT-NN] ..." --body-file ... --label sprint-task |
| Trigger review | gh issue edit <N> --add-label sonnet-review --repo eva-foundry/37-data-model |

---

## Sprint Boundary Rules

- Sprint N does not start until Sprint N-1 is merged and reviews are closed
- MTI gate: >= 95 (37-data-model is foundational -- higher bar than application projects)
- pytest gate: exits 0 -- no exceptions
- validate gate: violation_count = 0 -- no cross-reference failures
- Every modified file must have a matching EVA-STORY tag
- No sprint issue is created without a committed sprint manifest

---

## Story ID Invariant (never violate)

Every F37-NN-NNN ID is assigned exactly once by seed-from-plan.py.
The assignment is deterministic: stories are counted sequentially within their epic
in PLAN.md parse order (top to bottom).

Adding a story at the END of its epic is safe -- it gets the next sequential ID.
Inserting a story in the MIDDLE of an epic shifts all subsequent story IDs.
  -- Never insert in the middle of a production epic.
  -- Append new stories at the end of their epic / feature block.

reflect-ids.py will apply the new ID immediately after seeding.
PLAN.md becomes the ground truth -- no separate lookup is required.

---

## 37-Data-Model Specific Notes

**Project ID Prefix**: F37 (Feature 37 -- EVA Data Model)

**Epic Mapping**:
- F37-01: Foundation (services, personas, feature_flags)
- F37-02: Data/API Layers (containers, endpoints, schemas)
- F37-03: UI Layers (screens, literals, agents)
- F37-04: Requirements/Infrastructure (requirements, infrastructure)
- F37-05: Control Plane (planes, connections, environments, cp_skills, cp_agents, runbooks, cp_workflows, cp_policies)
- F37-06: Frontend Objects (components, hooks, ts_types)
- F37-07: Catalog (mcp_servers, prompts, security_controls)
- F37-08: Project Plane (projects, wbs)
- F37-09: DPDCA Layers (sprints, milestones, risks, decisions)
- F37-10: Portal Face (Browser UI for model explorer + reports)
- F37-FK: FK Enhancement (RelationshipMeta, temporal versioning, scenarios, IaC, pipelines)

**Test Command**: `pytest tests/ -x -q` (must exit 0)

**MTI Gate**: 95 (higher than apps because data model is foundational)

**Validate Gate**: `POST /model/admin/validate` must return `count: 0`

**Cosmos Export**: Always run `POST /model/admin/commit` in Act step (writes to Cosmos 24x7)

**ACA Deployment**: 37-data-model is deployed to ACA at:
  https://msub-eva-data-model.victoriousgrass-30debbd3.canadacentral.azurecontainerapps.io

**Local Dev**: Port 8010 (SQLite MemoryStore fallback)

**FK Enhancement Context**: When working on FK Enhancement stories (F37-FK-*), always read:
- docs/FK-ENHANCEMENT-COMPLETE-PLAN-2026-02-28.md (12 sprints, 403h)
- docs/FK-ENHANCEMENT-OPUS-FINDINGS-2026-02-28.md (Opus 4.6 verdict + fixes)
- docs/library/10-FK-ENHANCEMENT.md (agent-consumable summary)

**Relationship Types**: 27 edge types defined in api/graph.py (20 existing + 7 new for CI/CD)

**Store Abstraction**: AbstractStore interface supports MemoryStore (SQLite) and CosmosStore (ACA production)
